★★★★★ "Tremendous" - The Guardian; ★★★★★ "Pure joy" - The London Standard.

title: A View of One’s Own: Landscapes by British Women Artists, 1760-1860
desc: This exhibition showcases early landscape watercolours by British women artists, whose work represents a growing area of the Courtauld's Collection.

title: The Barber in London: Highlights from a Remarkable Collection
desc: A selection of exceptional paintings from The Barber Institute of Fine Arts is on display at the Courtauld Gallery until August 2026.

title: The Painted Tower: Conservation in Context at Longthorpe
desc: This display will showcase the pioneering role of the Courtauld's Conservation department in safeguarding Britain’s wall paintings and the peculiar challenges of caring for paintings attached to historic buildings.

title: Hepworth in Colour
desc: This ambitious exhibition will be the first to explore a less familiar aspect of Barbara Hepworth's (1903 –1975) work, the artist’s lifelong fascination with colour, which she used in highly original and unexpected ways.

title: Salman Toor: Someone Like You
desc: In Autumn 2026, the Courtauld will present the first solo exhibition in Europe of the celebrated New York-based painter Salman Toor, bringing together around 20 of the artist’s paintings.

title: Studio Prints: An Artists’ Workshop
desc: Discover the world of Dorothea Wight and Marc Balakjian’s London Printmaking Studio. Featuring prints by artists including Lucian Freud, Frank Auerbach and Paula Rego.

title: Hepworth and Nicholson: The Hampstead Studio Photographs
desc: Step behind the scenes of Barbara Hepworth and Ben Nicholson’s London Studio. This display coincides with the major exhibition Hepworth in Colour in Summer 2026.

title: Winifred Gill: A Bloomsbury Pioneer
desc: This display will spotlight the artist Winifred Gill (1891-1981), through works held in the Courtauld’s collection, including her inventive toy designs for the Omega Workshops.

desc: Nearly 20 years after The Devil Wears Prada, Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci return to Runway.

desc: Academy of Ancient Music joins The Wave Quartet in an ingenious, audacious musical voyage across five centuries.

title: Kokuho
desc: A tale of friendship and rivalry in the world of classical Japanese performance, this epic Japanese historical drama is inspired by director Lee Sang-il's obsession with kabuki theatre.

title: Rose of Nevada
desc: Thirty years after it was lost with all hands, a fishing boat reappears in its old harbour. Arthouse Cornish filmmaker Mark Jenkin creates an eerie coastal world lost in time.

desc: Colombian artist Delcy Morelos explores our relationship with the earth in a monumental, site-specific installation made of soil, clay and fragrant spices.

desc: Join artist Delcy Morelos and curator Diego Chocano for a conversation and Q&A about Morelos’ practice and her new Barbican commission, origo.

title: Nederlands Kamerkoor: Forgotten
desc: Nederlands Kamerkoor bring to the Barbican their compelling new project Forgotten – a moving musical and theatrical exploration of memory loss.

desc: An enchanting feature-length animation made entirely from cut paper.

desc: A leading voice in Spanish arthouse cinema, Carla Simón’s third feature completes her semi-autobiographical trilogy, a body of work rooted in memory and her own upbringing.

title: Visit the Conservatory   Free
desc: Visit the second biggest Conservatory in London and explore our hidden tropical oasis.

desc: A collection of short-form documentaries connected to yarn – from dyeing, weaving, quilting, lacemaking to embroidery.

title: David August: Solo Piano
desc: The Italian-German electronic composer performs his intimate and restrained new solo piano album, HYMNS.

desc: Anne-Sophie Mutter will celebrate her 50th anniversary on stage in 2026 and 2027, presenting a programme alongside fellow trio musicians Maximilian Hornung and Lauma Skride

desc: A tale of friendship and rivalry in the world of classical Japanese performance, this epic historical drama is inspired by director Lee Sang-il's obsession with kabuki theatre.

desc: The BBC Symphony Orchestra presents Requiem for America: Singing for the Invisible People — Brent Michael Davids’s searing work which confronts hard truths about America’s founding.

title: Parent & Baby Screening: Kokuho
desc: A tale of friendship and rivalry in the world of classical Japanese performance, this epic Japanese historical drama is inspired by director Lee Sang-il's obsession with kabuki theatre.

title: Relaxed Screening: Sirât
desc: Oliver Laxe’s mystical odyssey in the desert is propelled by hallucinatory visuals and thumping beats.

title: Architecture on Stage: Asli Çiçek
desc: Aslı Çiçek joins us to discuss her practice at the intersection of architecture and scenography.

title: Tangram: Divine Intimacies
desc: Inspired by one of the earliest texts of Chinese philosophy, Divine Intimacies explores how nearly a thousand years of musical thought can be translated into contemporary sound.

title: Cole Porter: High Society
desc: You're invited to the most swell party of the year as this summer's eagerly anticipated production delivers another glorious dose of Golden Age musical theatre escapism.

title: Preschool Pics: Amazing Animations + Music with Tara Franks
desc: Get your groove on in our cinema with a fun music play session and a selection of short films, curated with our youngest audience members in mind!

desc: Kick-start your evening with a 60-minute Half Six Fix concert, showcasing Mahler's Fourth Symphony, introduced by the performers and with screens in the hall to bring you closer to the action.

title: Queer East Festival: A Useful Ghost
desc: In this wildly camp debut that's both political and joyous, a ghost has returned as a vacuum cleaner.

title: In Other Worlds
desc: Our new immersive exhibition invites you to step into the future.

title: London Symphony Orchestra/Sir Simon Rattle
desc: Serenity, beauty and the sense of an ending infuse music by Richard Strauss and Mahler, preluded by Gerhard’s immersive soundscapes.

title: Guildhall Jazz Orchestra: Porgy and Bess
desc: Guildhall Jazz Orchestra welcomes internationally acclaimed trumpet soloist Ingrid Jensen for a performance of Miles Davis and Gil Evans’ luminous 1958  reimagining of the Gershwins' Porgy and Bess.

desc: Mastercard priceless.com ticket holders are invited to step into the future, at the Barbican's new immersive exhibition.

title: Guildhall School: As You Like It

title: Making America: Oramo conducts Bartók & Korngold
desc: Strangers in a strange land: Sakari Oramo conducts music by Bartók and Korngold, European composers stranded in America and dreaming of a better world.

title: Family Film Club: James and the Giant Peach
desc: See Roald Dahl's beloved, wacky tale of a young boy and his unusual group of friends, celebrating its 20th anniversary on the big screen.

title: London Symphony Chorus
desc: Celebrate the London Symphony Chorus’s 60th Anniversary with Duruflé’s Requiem and two world premieres, conducted by Mariana Rosas.

title: London Symphony Orchestra/Sir Simon Rattle
desc: Sir Simon Rattle conducts four powerful moments from the final chapter of Wagner's epic Ring cycle, alongside his Siegfried Idyll.

title: Senior Community Cinema: Sentimental Value
desc: A marvellous and complex family drama from Joachim Trier, where a film-director reunites with his estranged daughter.

title: Midge Ure
desc: A curated live experience that reimagines Midge Ure's career as one flowing narrative. Celebrating over 40 years of innovation, from glam and punk to synthpop and orchestral textures.

title: Architecture on Stage: Neil Gillespie, Reiach and Hall
desc: In a talk entitled In Two Minds, the practice's director Neil Gillespie turns his attention to a curated handful of Reiach and Hall's projects that carry particular personal meaning for him.

title: Rickie Lee Jones
desc: 'The Duchess of Coolsville' (Time magazine) returns to the Hall with her latest tour, promising an enchanting evening of music and storytelling, as Jones shares her classics and personal favourites.

title: Kaitlyn Aurelia Smith
desc: The electronic music composer and synthesist invites us to experience new music alongside key moments from across her catalogue, in a setting designed for intensity, scale and close listening.

title: NT Live: The Playboy of the Western World
desc: This riveting story of youth and self-discovery turns Pegeen Flaherty's life upside down after a man walks into her pub claiming he's killed his father.

title: Relaxed Screening: Project Hail Mary
desc: Ryan Gosling is a desperate astronaut on a mission to save the future, based on the sci-fi novel by the author of The Martian.

title: Aldous Harding
desc: After two sold-out shows in 2023, the singer-songwriter returns with music from her new album, Train on the Island.

title: Family Film Club: Arco
desc: A dazzling new animated sci-fi that looks to the future with hope.

title: Experiments in Film: A Focus on Gabriel Abrantes: Diamantino

title: MET Opera Live in HD: El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego

desc: An unruly collection of cinematic oddities and satirical sketches hosted by Ratzo – a mischievous virtual rat and alter ego of filmmaker Gabriel Abrantes.

title: Le Poème Harmonique: Allegri's Miserere
desc: Become immersed in the sounds of a different world with French ensemble Le Poème Harmonique, exploring the mystical, ceremonial energy at the heart of music from the Counter Reformation.

title: Djrum

title: Spatial, No Problem Version Part One
desc: Mouse on Mars, together with collaborators Dodo NKishi and Louis Chude-Sokei, present a real time remix of their collaboration with the late, great dub legend Lee "Scratch" Perry.

desc: Spectacular cinematic scores from some of the biggest and most celebrated movies of all time – all originally recorded by the London Symphony Orchestra.
